name: System Design 101
tagline: Beautiful, popular, and among the most restrictive here
description: Visual explainers of system-design concepts — APIs, databases, caching, distributed patterns — with real-world case studies, aimed at interview prep, from the ByteByteGo newsletter team.
why: Gorgeous and widely shared, but licensed CC BY-NC-ND: no commercial use and no derivatives. Despite the open-repo optics it's among the most restrictive licenses in this catalog, and it doubles as marketing for a paid newsletter.
useCases: A visual reference for architecture concepts
audience: Engineers prepping interviews; juniors building mental models

license.code: CC BY-NC-ND-4.0 (not OSI; no commercial use, no derivatives)

limitations: You can't remix, translate, or reuse it commercially without permission, and the depth is intentionally introductory.
take: No-derivatives is the license clause most people never read. Here it means you may look, but you may not build on it.
